items.title: Cyclone Narelle's destructive path across Australia, in pictures and video

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

gallery.altText: Damaged crops in a banana plantation

thumbnail.altText: Damaged crops in a banana plantation

items.title: These 'readers' created in remote NT are unlike those in most schools

children.content: More than 40 years ago, a program was created to help Papunya children in remote NT learn to read in Pintupi-Luritja languages. Those books are now an insight into the people, stories, history and culture of the Papunya and are on display at the National Library of Australia.

image.altText: A yellow book cover that reads "Ngaatja Nyaalytjingka?".

items.title: A 'rare parasitic fungus' is infecting Top End footballers. Here's what we know

children.content: Health authorities say a rare water-borne pathogen is behind the recent outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End football clubs, with a number of Darwin playing fields deemed high-risk infection sites.

image.altText: a man taking a mark in a football game, splashing in mud.

items.title: Australians mark Good Friday with seafood, church services and a boat race

children.content: Australians begin the long weekend with church services, time outdoors and seafood-filled lunches.

items.title: Population of NT town doubles overnight as flood evacuees arrive

children.content: Flood evacuees from Daly River and Palumpa have been transferred from Darwin to the small town of Batchelor, where they could stay for months. Locals say they were not consulted before the town's population doubled overnight.

image.altText: An aerial image of a small town.

items.title: 'Extraordinary' images of home as astronauts head towards moon

children.content: As Artemis II astronauts forge ahead, NASA has released new images from inside the Orion spacecraft, including a portrait of Earth's deep blue oceans.

articles.title: This moon mission is different. They have toilets now

children.content: More than 50 years ago, the Apollo missions took humanity to the Moon. Now with Artemis, a new generation is about to be launched. What has changed — and not changed — between then and now might surprise you.

image.altText: Black and white photo of woman with long hair, glasses and a long-sleeved dress next to a stack of bound papers as tall as her.

gallery.altText: The entire Earth is seen from space, with blue oceans and swirling tendrils of white clouds.
This image shows a curved slice of Earth in a space capsule window.
Three men and one woman are floating in zero gravity chatting on a video call, dressed in navy NASA tops.

thumbnail.altText: The entire Earth is seen from space, with blue oceans and swirling tendrils of white clouds.
This image shows a curved slice of Earth in a space capsule window.
Three men and one woman are floating in zero gravity chatting on a video call, dressed in navy NASA tops.

items.title: Strategically critical Pacific island divided over growing US military presence

children.content: The Pacific Island of Guam sits at the centre of a struggle between the world's most powerful military and the right of its people to determine their own future.

items.title: Sunken trawler alerts police to alleged drug-smuggling plan

children.content: Police have charged nine men over a scheme to allegedly smuggle tonnes of cocaine into Victoria by sea.

image.altText: A man in handcuffs with police.

items.title: The loopholes to look for if you’re considering downgrading your private health cover

children.content: The loopholes to look for if you're thinking about downgrading or changing your private health insurance.

image.altText: A graphic which has health care cards on it.

items.title: Who is eligible for the new nasal spray flu vaccine?

children.content: It's been available in the northern hemisphere for years, now Australian children can receive a nasal spray vaccine for the flu.

image.altText: A small child receives a nasal spray while sitting on a parent's lap

items.title: How a rising teen star is making a name for himself in Aussie athletics

children.content: Teenagers Gout Gout and Cameron Myers are already creating an impact on the international stage, helping to inspire the likes of 18-year-old Daniel Williams to follow in their footsteps.

gallery.altText: Daniel Williams takes off his race bib to give to spectators at the 2026 Maurie Plant Meet.

thumbnail.altText: Daniel Williams takes off his race bib to give to spectators at the 2026 Maurie Plant Meet.

items.title: World-famous Bibbulmun Track considered for heritage list

children.content: Councils support moves for the Bibbulmun Track to be declared a heritage-listed site, which conservationists hope could protect the track from future mining expansions.

image.altText: Hikers and karri trees on the Bibbulmun Track

items.title: Study says economic value of grey-headed flying fox poo worth millions

children.content: Bats are often thought of as smelly, ugly and noisy, but a study has revealed the economic and ecological value of one Australian species's poo.

image.altText: A grey-headed flying fox hanging from an aviary roof eating from a pink plant as it hangs upside down.

items.title: How do you clean a toilet brush and when should you change it?

children.content: If you're just shoving the brush into its holder to wait for the next use and actively avoiding thinking about how dirty the whole concept of it is, you're not alone. Here's how to make sure your toilet brush is as hygienic as possible while it lasts.

gallery.altText: A close-up of a person's hand inside a red glove, holding a toilet brush to a toilet bowl

thumbnail.altText: A close-up of a person's hand inside a red glove, holding a toilet brush to a toilet bowl

avatar.alt: A headshot of a woman with long light brown hair and blue eyes

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of Top Gun, bowling underarm and your weight on the Moon

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: Goose and Maverick from the film Top Gun

items.title: Gordi helped write a song for her partner, then 'stole it' back

children.content: Gordi's new single was originally meant to be for her partner. Thankfully, both artists know the importance of trusting their gut.

image.altText: Gordi wearing a shiny jacket and holding the collar while standing against a dark background with pink lighting.

items.title: From farm to fashion festival: Indigenous designs stun on the runway

children.content: When a farmer and a First Nations artist join forces, something beautiful emerges — thanks to Japanese knitting technology.

gallery.altText: a woman walking down a runway wearing a long white dress featuring a feather waist.
man wearing blue coat, woman wearing short green dress, woman wearing long teal dress, woman wearing brown top and pants
Elderly woman sewing red material with white sewing machine, smiling

man wearing blue coat, woman wearing short green dress, woman wearing long teal dress, woman wearing brown top and pants

items.title: Lakers lose 'MVP candidate' to injury as NBA play-offs approach

children.content: The Los Angeles Lakers are dealt a major blow, with one of their key stars ruled out indefinitely.

image.altText: Luka Doncic playing for the La Lakers during the 2025/26 NBA season.

items.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status

children.content: The Liberal Party will have just one more seat than One Nation in South Australia’s lower house, leaving opposition leader Ashton Hurn little margin for error.

image.altText: composite image of Cory Bernardi outdoors and Ashton Hurn in a dark room, both looking serious

items.title: 'Adrenaline rush' of adaptive skateboarding redefines inclusive sport

children.content: Using a specially designed skate frame, adaptive skateboarding allows participants to stand safely while trained instructors guide and stabilise them.

image.altText: two people skateboarding using a harness.

items.title: Voss unfazed about mounting speculation over his future as Blues coach

children.content: Michael Voss knows he faces more scrutiny over his role as Blues coach after his side endures another second-half meltdown.

image.altText: Michael Voss waits for a reporter's question at an AFL post-match media conference.

items.title: I find myself weeping again for Lebanon

children.content: Lebanese families have been told to flee homes that they might never be able to return to, and families are setting up tents for an uncertain and dangerous life on the streets of Beirut all because of a conflict not of their making, brought about by another state.

gallery.altText: Women walk by a damaged building in Beirut.

thumbnail.altText: Women walk by a damaged building in Beirut.

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing elected president

children.content: In a live broadcast of the vote count the former commander-in-chief comfortably passed the threshold required to win the presidency.

items.title: Bellamy threatens to wield axe after Storm's humiliating loss to Panthers

children.content: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy puts his players on notice after they were thrashed 50-10 by Penrith on Good Friday.

image.altText: Cameron Munster and Storm teammates re-group during their NRL loss to the Panthers.

items.title: After decades of success, love and loss, outback pub changes hands

children.content: After turning "the worst pub you ever walked into" into a tourism award winner, Stephen Burns is stepping away from managing the Nindigully Pub.

gallery.altText: An ariel view of the pub with some four wheel rives out front and campervans down the side.
An ariel view of the pub with some four wheel rives out front and campervans down the side.
The Nindigully pub painted completely pink.
